tools.description: Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created. Labels ["test-reliability"] will be automatically added.

body.description: Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

labels.description: Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.

parent.description: Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.

title.description: Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

method.description:
Action to specify what pull request data needs to be retrieved from GitHub. 
Possible options: 
 1. get - Get details of a specific pull request.
 2. get_diff - Get the diff of a pull request.
 3. get_status - Get combined commit status of a head commit in a pull request.
 4. get_files - Get the list of files changed in a pull request. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 5. get_review_comments - Get review threads on a pull request. Each thread contains logically grouped review comments made on the same code location during pull request reviews. Returns threads with metadata (isResolved, isOutdated, isCollapsed) and their associated comments. Use cursor-based pagination (perPage, after) to control results.
 6. get_reviews - Get the reviews on a pull request. When asked for review comments, use get_review_comments method.
 7. get_comments - Get comments on a pull request. Use this if user doesn't specifically want review comments. Use with pagination parameters to control the number of results returned.
 8. get_check_runs - Get check runs for the head commit of a pull request. Check runs are the individual CI/CD jobs and checks that run on the PR.

{"number":37864,"title":"Tracking: Known pre-existing CI test failures","body":"## Purpose

This issue tracks **known, pre-existing CI test failures** that are caused by infrastructure, service-side, or external dependency issues — not by code bugs in this repository. The automated [Analyze CI Test Failures](.github/workflows/fix-test-failures.md) workflow uses this issue to skip known failures and avoid filing duplicate issues.

## Known Failures

_No known CI (playback) failures at this time._

> **Note:** This list only tracks failures from **CI pipelines** (`ci.yml`, playback mode). Live-test pipeline failures (`tests.yml`) are excluded — the workflow filters them out by check-run name.

## Maintenance Guidelines

- **Adding entries:** When a CI failure is confirmed to be a pre-existing infrastructure/service issue (not a code bug), add it here with the package name, test pattern, error summary, root cause, and a tracking issue link.
- **Removing entries:** When the underlying issue is fixed and CI is green again, remove the entry and close the corresponding tracking issue.
- **Format:** Each entry must include the **package name** (as it appears in check-run names) so the workflow can match it.
- **Review cadence:** Review this list monthly to check if any known failures have been resolved.

{"total_count":2,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4335130819,"number":38297,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"CI failures on main: `@azure/arm-computelimit`, `@azure/openai`, `@azure/arm-springappdiscovery`","body":"## Summary Three CI pipeline check runs on the `main` branch are failing as of the HEAD commit [`0dbfbadd`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (merged 2026-04-27). All three failures are rooted in **CI infrastructure issues** rather than code bugs: two are caused by an outdated Ubuntu-24.04 CI image (47 days old, threshold 45 days), and one is a macOS runner network error preventing Azure credential acquisition. --- ## Failures ### `@azure/arm-computelimit` — Build Analyze **Check run:** `js - computelimit - mgmt (Build Analyze)` (id: 73173901636, completed 2026-04-27T10:39Z) **Failing tests:** - No unit tests — this is a Build Analyze (API Extractor / lint) job **Error:** ```` 1ES PT Non-Blocking Error: Image ubuntu-24.04 with version 130.0.0 is 47 days old (>= 45 days). Your pipeline may fail due to dependency issues on or after 2026-05-10. Please refresh your managed image or contact your image owners/infra team. --- PowerShell exited with code '1'. ``` **Root cause:** The Ubuntu-24.04 managed image used by this CI pipeline is 47 days old and has exceeded the 45-day freshness threshold enforced by the 1ES Platform. The stale image is causing tool dependency failures that make the PowerShell build/analyze script exit with code 1. **Suggested fix:** This requires an **infrastructure action**, not a code change. The managed image pool for `ubuntu-24.04` needs to be refreshed by the infra/platform team before 2026-05-10 to avoid further failures. See: (aka.ms/redacted) **Reproduced locally:** No (infrastructure issue; local builds are unaffected) --- ### `@azure/openai` — Build **Check run:** `js - openai (Build Build)` (id: 73170438989, completed 2026-04-27T10:15Z) **Failing tests:** - No unit tests — this is a Build (compile + pack) job **Error:** ``` 1ES PT Non-Blocking Error: Image ubuntu-24.04 with version 130.0.0 is 47 days old (>= 45 days). Your pipeline may fail due to dependency issues on or after 2026-05-10. --- Bash exited with code '2'. Manifest generation failed, please check output file for more info. Path does not exist: /mnt/vss/_work/_temp/packages-dev-publish Script failed with exit code: 1 ``` **Root cause:** The same stale Ubuntu-24.04 image (47 days old) is causing the build script to fail with exit code 2. As a cascade, `npm pack` never runs and the dev-publish staging directory (`/mnt/vss/_work/_temp/packages-dev-publish`) is never created, causing the manifest generation step to fail. This is not a TypeScript or code error in the package itself — the source compiles correctly; the failure originates in the CI image's toolchain environment. **Suggested fix:** Same infrastructure image refresh as for `@azure/arm-computelimit` above. No code change required. **Reproduced locally:** No (infrastructure issue; local builds are unaffected) --- ### `@azure/arm-springappdiscovery` — Unit Tests (macOS) **Check run:** `js - springappdiscovery - mgmt (Build UnitTest macoslatest_24x_node)` (id: 73155915099, completed 2026-04-27T08:36Z) **Failing tests:** - All tests (the test-proxy never started, so no tests ran) **Error:** ``` azure-sdk-npm-token=[REDACTED] not fetch access token for Azure. Status code: network_error, status message: Network request failed" js-turborepo-cache-token=[REDACTED] not fetch access token for Azure. Status code: network_error, status message: Network request failed" PowerShell exited with code '1'. Path does not exist: /Users/runner/work/1/a/test-proxy.log ```` **Root cause:** The macOS (`macoslatest_24x_node`) runner failed to acquire Azure credentials at pipeline startup — both the npm-feed token and the Turborepo cache token fetch returned `network_error`. Because credential acquisition failed, the test-proxy process was never started (its log file was never written), and the entire unit-test job was aborted before any test code ran. This is an intermittent macOS runner network or Azure identity endpoint connectivity issue unrelated to the package's code. **Suggested fix:** This requires an **infrastructure/runner investigation**. No code change is needed for `@azure/arm-springappdiscovery`. The job can be re-run once the macOS runner network connectivity issue is resolved. **Reproduced locally:** No (infrastructure/network issue) --- ## Additional Notes - All three failures were detected on commit [`0dbfbadd`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (2026-04-27). - The Ubuntu-24.04 image age issue (`computelimit`, `openai`) is systemic and will affect **all** CI pipelines using that image pool. The 1ES PT warning notes that failures will worsen on or after **2026-05-10** if the image is not refreshed. - No pre-existing failures were excluded (the known-failures tracking issue #37864 currently has no entries). - None of these failures indicate regressions in the TypeScript source code; they are purely CI infrastructure issues. > Generated by [Analyze CI Test {"total_count":7,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":4335130819,"number":38297,"state":"open","locked":false,"title":"CI failures on main: `@azure/arm-computelimit`, `@azure/openai`, `@azure/arm-springappdiscovery`","body":"## Summary

Three CI pipeline check runs on the `main` branch are failing as of the HEAD commit [`0dbfbadd`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (merged 2026-04-27). All three failures are rooted in **CI infrastructure issues** rather than code bugs: two are caused by an outdated Ubuntu-24.04 CI image (47 days old, threshold 45 days), and one is a macOS runner network error preventing Azure credential acquisition.

---

## Failures

### `@azure/arm-computelimit` — Build Analyze

**Check run:** `js - computelimit - mgmt (Build Analyze)` (id: 73173901636, completed 2026-04-27T10:39Z)

**Failing tests:**
- No unit tests — this is a Build Analyze (API Extractor / lint) job

**Error:**
````
1ES PT Non-Blocking Error: Image ubuntu-24.04 with version 130.0.0 is 47 days old
(>= 45 days). Your pipeline may fail due to dependency issues on or after 2026-05-10.
Please refresh your managed image or contact your image owners/infra team.
---
PowerShell exited with code '1'.
```

**Root cause:**
The Ubuntu-24.04 managed image used by this CI pipeline is 47 days old and has exceeded the 45-day freshness threshold enforced by the 1ES Platform. The stale image is causing tool dependency failures that make the PowerShell build/analyze script exit with code 1.

**Suggested fix:**
This requires an **infrastructure action**, not a code change. The managed image pool for `ubuntu-24.04` needs to be refreshed by the infra/platform team before 2026-05-10 to avoid further failures. See: (aka.ms/redacted)

**Reproduced locally:** No (infrastructure issue; local builds are unaffected)

---

### `@azure/openai` — Build

**Check run:** `js - openai (Build Build)` (id: 73170438989, completed 2026-04-27T10:15Z)

**Failing tests:**
- No unit tests — this is a Build (compile + pack) job

**Error:**
```
1ES PT Non-Blocking Error: Image ubuntu-24.04 with version 130.0.0 is 47 days old
(>= 45 days). Your pipeline may fail due to dependency issues on or after 2026-05-10.
---
Bash exited with code '2'.
Manifest generation failed, please check output file for more info.
Path does not exist: /mnt/vss/_work/_temp/packages-dev-publish
Script failed with exit code: 1
```

**Root cause:**
The same stale Ubuntu-24.04 image (47 days old) is causing the build script to fail with exit code 2. As a cascade, `npm pack` never runs and the dev-publish staging directory (`/mnt/vss/_work/_temp/packages-dev-publish`) is never created, causing the manifest generation step to fail. This is not a TypeScript or code error in the package itself — the source compiles correctly; the failure originates in the CI image's toolchain environment.

**Suggested fix:**
Same infrastructure image refresh as for `@azure/arm-computelimit` above. No code change required.

**Reproduced locally:** No (infrastructure issue; local builds are unaffected)

---

### `@azure/arm-springappdiscovery` — Unit Tests (macOS)

**Check run:** `js - springappdiscovery - mgmt (Build UnitTest macoslatest_24x_node)` (id: 73155915099, completed 2026-04-27T08:36Z)

**Failing tests:**
- All tests (the test-proxy never started, so no tests ran)

**Error:**
```
azure-sdk-npm-token=[REDACTED] not fetch access token for Azure.
  Status code: network_error, status message: Network request failed"
js-turborepo-cache-token=[REDACTED] not fetch access token for Azure.
  Status code: network_error, status message: Network request failed"
PowerShell exited with code '1'.
Path does not exist: /Users/runner/work/1/a/test-proxy.log
````

**Root cause:**
The macOS (`macoslatest_24x_node`) runner failed to acquire Azure credentials at pipeline startup — both the npm-feed token and the Turborepo cache token fetch returned `network_error`. Because credential acquisition failed, the test-proxy process was never started (its log file was never written), and the entire unit-test job was aborted before any test code ran. This is an intermittent macOS runner network or Azure identity endpoint connectivity issue unrelated to the package's code.

**Suggested fix:**
This requires an **infrastructure/runner investigation**. No code change is needed for `@azure/arm-springappdiscovery`. The job can be re-run once the macOS runner network connectivity issue is resolved.

**Reproduced locally:** No (infrastructure/network issue)

---

## Additional Notes

- All three failures were detected on commit [`0dbfbadd`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (2026-04-27).
- The Ubuntu-24.04 image age issue (`computelimit`, `openai`) is systemic and will affect **all** CI pipelines using that image pool. The 1ES PT warning notes that failures will worsen on or after **2026-05-10** if the image is not refreshed.
- No pre-existing failures were excluded (the known-failures tracking issue #37864 currently has no entries).
- None of these failures indicate regressions in the TypeScript source code; they are purely CI infrastructure issues.

test failures on main: `@azure/arm-kubernetesconfiguration`","body":"## Summary

1 package affected, 1 test failing as of CI build `#20260410.1` (commit [`41b10c38`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]), 2026-04-10). The test recorder fails to start a playback session with the test proxy, causing the `operation list test` in `@azure/arm-kubernetesconfiguration` to fail. This is a **new** failure (1 new, 0 recurring) that first appeared on the same commit that added `arm-kubernetesconfiguration-extensions` to the `js - kubernetesconfiguration - mgmt` CI pipeline (PR #38018).

## Failures

### `@azure/arm-kubernetesconfiguration`

**Failing tests:**
- `kubernetesconfiguration test > operation list test` in `sdk/kubernetesconfiguration/arm-kubernetesconfiguration/test/kubernetesconfiguration_operations_test.spec.ts`

**Check run:** `js - kubernetesconfiguration - mgmt (Build UnitTest macoslatest_24x_node)` → [run 70757322962](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/runs/70757322962)

**CI test summary:** Failed: 1 (1 new, 0 recurring) / Passed: 6 / Other: 12 / Total: 19

**Error:**
```
RecorderError: Start request failed.
 ❯ Recorder.start ../../test-utils/recorder/src/recorder.ts:336:19
 ❯ test/kubernetesconfiguration_operations_test.spec.ts:41:5

Serialized Error: { statusCode: undefined }
```

**Root cause:**

The `Recorder.start()` call in `beforeEach` POSTs to the test proxy to begin a playback session. The test proxy responded with a non-200 HTTP status (the status code is not propagated into the `RecorderError` constructor, so it surfaces as `statusCode: undefined`). This is a **test-infrastructure failure**, not a code or test-logic bug:

- The recording file **exists** in `Azure/azure-sdk-assets` at tag `js/kubernetesconfiguration/arm-kubernetesconfiguration_615562201f`, path `js/sdk/kubernetesconfiguration/arm-kubernetesconfiguration/recordings/node/kubernetesconfiguration_test/recording_operation_list_test.json`.
- The recording format and content are valid (single `GET /providers/Microsoft.KubernetesConfiguration/operations?api-version=2023-05-01` → 200 response with a `value` array of 27 operations).
- 6 other tests passed in the same CI run, confirming the test proxy **was** running for part of the pipeline.
- The failure is classified as **1 new, 0 recurring**, indicating it appeared for the first time on this specific CI run.
- The triggering commit (`41b10c38` / PR #38018) added `arm-kubernetesconfiguration-extensions` to `ci.mgmt.yml` for the first time, expanding the set of packages tested in a single CI run from 4 to 5.

The most likely explanation is a test proxy crash or resource-contention issue on the macOS Node 24 CI runner when managing asset-sync checkout across the newly expanded set of 5 packages. The test proxy must switch between asset tags (`arm-kubernetesconfiguration_615562201f` and the newly added `arm-kubernetesconfiguration-extensions_e78462a13b`) during the run, which may have caused a state issue.

**Suggested fix:**

No code change is required — the recording and source are correct. The recommended investigation steps are:

1. **Re-trigger the CI pipeline** on `main` to determine whether this is a transient failure. If it passes on a re-run, add the test to a flakiness watchlist.
2. **Check test proxy asset-sync behavior** when multiple packages share a service directory and have different asset tags. Specifically, verify the test proxy correctly isolates the working directory for each package's `assets.json` tag when running sequentially.
3. If the failure recurs, **check macOS Node 24 test proxy logs** in Azure DevOps build `#20260410.1` for the `Build UnitTest macoslatest_24x_node` job to see the exact HTTP status code returned by the test proxy to `recorder.start()`.

**Reproduced locally:** No (test proxy binary download fails in sandboxed network environment; the recording and source code are structurally correct)

---

## Additional Notes

**`js - openai (Build Build)` failure excluded:** The openai CI pipeline explicitly sets `RunUnitTests: false` (see `sdk/openai/ci.yml`). Its "Build Build" failure (`Manifest generation failed` / `Path does not exist: /mnt/vss/_work/_temp/packages-dev-publish`) is a CI packaging/dev-publish infrastructure failure, not a unit test failure, and is therefore out of scope for this issue.

[Content truncated due to length]","body":"## Summary

CI checks on `main` at commit `[REDACTED]` (2026-03-23) show failures across **6 packages**: `@azure/communication-identity`, `@azure/storage-blob`, `@azure/communication-short-codes`, `@azure/openai`, `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence`, and `@azure/arm-changeanalysis`. The test failures (4 packages) are all caused by live-infrastructure issues (Azure service errors, rate limits, and storage account policy changes), while 2 packages have build/analyze pipeline failures with no test failures. Build #20260323.1 is the CI run inspected.

---

## Failures

### `@azure/communication-identity`

**Failing tests:**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live]` (live test)
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] [AAD]` (live test)

**Error:**
````
invalid_grant: AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available.
If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: 824d187e-7336-4f7c-884f-94c520ec0500
```

**Root cause:**
Azure Active Directory returned error code `5000224` indicating the target resource is temporarily unavailable on the Microsoft backend. This is a live-test-only failure affecting 12 tests (all recurring, 0 new). No code change triggered this failure.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change required. This is a service-side availability issue. The Azure Identity team should be notified if the failure persists. The affected tests should be retried once the service recovers.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure credentials and service availability)

---

### `@azure/storage-blob`

**Failing tests:**
- `BlobServiceClient > createContainer and deleteContainer`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlockFromURL copy source blob as single block`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlockFromURL copy source blob as separate blocks`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlock, stageBlockURL and commitBlockList with CPK`
- (14 additional related tests)

**Error:**
```
Public access is not permitted on this storage account.
RequestId:84e2538d-501e-0057-22a3-bae3f7000000
Time:2026-03-23T08:57:34.1891632Z
````

**Root cause:**
The test storage account's public blob access policy was changed (or a new account was provisioned with public access disabled by default). The test `createContainer and deleteContainer` explicitly sets `access: "container"` to create a public container; the live test run against the current storage account rejects this with `PublicAccessNotPermitted`. All 18 failures are **new** (0 recurring), which strongly indicates a recent test-environment infrastructure change rather than a code regression.

The specific test code:
````typescript
// sdk/storage/storage-blob/test/blobserviceclient.spec.ts, line ~422
const access = "container";
const { containerClient } = await blobServiceClient.createContainer(containerName, {
  access,
  metadata,
});
```

**Suggested fix:**
No source code change is needed. The test environment should be updated to use a storage account that permits public access, or the tests should be reviewed to determine if they can be run against an account without public access enabled. Ticket should be filed with the EngSys/test-resources team to restore or replace the storage account configuration.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure storage account with public access enabled)

---

### `@azure/communication-short-codes`

**Failing tests:**
- `ShortCodesClient - manage Attachments > can manage Attachments`

**Error:**
```
The server encountered an internal error.
```

**Root cause:**
The Communication Short Codes service is returning HTTP 500 Internal Server Errors for the Attachments management API. This is a recurring failure (7 tests, 0 new) indicating an ongoing service-side issue, not a code regression.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change required. The Azure Communication Services Short Codes team should be engaged to investigate the persistent server error in the Attachments API.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure Communication Services endpoint)

---

### `@azure/openai`

**Failing tests:**
- `Whisper > [2024-10-21] Client > audio.transcriptions.create > returns json transcription for mp3 files`
- `Whisper > [2024-10-21] Client > audio.transcriptions.create > returns json transcription for mp4 files`
- `Whisper > [2024-10-21] Client > audio.translations.create > returns json translation for mp3 files`
- `Whisper > [2024-10-21] Client > audio.translations.create > returns json translation for mp4 files`
- (336 additional failures)

**Error:**
```
429 Your requests to whisper for whisper in Sweden Central have exceeded the call rate limit
for your current OpenAI S0 pricing tier. This request was for Audio_Transcriptions under
Azure OpenAI API version 2024-10-21. Please retry after 30 seconds.
```

**Root cause:**
The Azure OpenAI Whisper deployment in the Sweden Central region has hit its rate limit (S0 pricing tier TPM/RPM quota). 340 total tests failed (28 new), caused by the live test suite exhausting the quota. This is a test-infrastructure resource allocation issue, not a code bug.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change needed. Options include:
1. Increase the Whisper deployment quota/capacity in the Sweden Central test environment.
2. Add retry logic with back-off to the test utilities for 429 responses (already using `maxRetriesOption` but the retries may be exhausting within the test timeout).
3. Consider running Whisper tests in a separate region or pipeline with adequate quota.

**Reproduced locally:** No (requires live Azure OpenAI endpoint with rate limits)

5 packages have failing CI check runs on the HEAD commit of `main` ([`4adaf9f`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]), 2026-03-18). The failures are distributed across `@azure/communication-identity` (12 tests), `@azure/storage-blob` (9 tests — all new), `@azure/communication-short-codes` (7 tests), `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence` (build-step failure, 0 test failures), and `@azure/openai` (311 tests, 295 recurring). All failures originate from **live service or infrastructure issues**, not from logic regressions in the package source code itself.

## Failures

### `@azure/communication-identity`

**Failing tests (12, all recurring):**
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live] [AAD]` — multiple platforms
- `Get Token For Teams User [Playback/Live]` — multiple platforms

**Error:**
```
AADSTS5000224: We are sorry, this resource is not available.
If you are seeing this message by mistake, please contact Microsoft support.
Trace ID: ea12d67a-763d-407f-8bd2-85cbfcfb2a00
```

**Root cause:**
The Azure Active Directory tenant/resource used by the CI service principal for Teams-user token tests returns `AADSTS5000224`, indicating the associated AAD resource is unavailable (possibly deprovisioned or misconfigured in the test environment). This is a live-mode test that calls real Azure AD endpoints; the error is 100% service-side and **does not point to any code bug**. All 12 failures are "recurring," meaning the issue predates the current HEAD.

**Suggested fix:**
No code change is needed. The CI service principal's Teams-user resource must be re-provisioned or the test environment reconfigured by the Azure Communication Services team. Consider adding a `skipEnv` / `isLiveMode` guard in the test to skip gracefully when the resource is unavailable, rather than failing:

````diff
--- a/sdk/communication/communication-identity/test/...spec.ts
+++ b/sdk/communication/communication-identity/test/...spec.ts
@@ ...
-  it("Get Token For Teams User", async () => {
+  it("Get Token For Teams User", async function() {
+    if (!isLiveMode()) { this.skip(); } // Teams token tests require live AAD resource
     ...
  });
```
_(Exact file path to be confirmed by the package team; this is illustrative.)_

**Reproduced locally:** No — requires live AAD credentials and a provisioned Teams resource.

### `@azure/storage-blob`

**Failing tests (9, all new):**
- `BlobServiceClient > createContainer and deleteContainer`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlockFromURL copy source blob as single block`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlock, stageBlockURL and commitBlockList with CPK`
- `BlockBlobClient > stageBlockFromURL copy source blob as separate blocks`
- (5 additional related tests)

**Error:**
```
Public access is not permitted on this storage account.
RequestId: 7d29b1b7-b01e-00ab-10b4-b6320e000000
Time: 2026-03-18T08:55:16.0408214Z
````

**Root cause:**
The tests explicitly set **public (anonymous) access** on test containers:

- `blobserviceclient.spec.ts` calls `blobServiceClient.createContainer(containerName, { access: "container" })`.
- `blockblobclient.spec.ts` calls `containerClient.setAccessPolicy("container")`.

The CI storage account has recently been configured to **disallow public blob access** at the account level (a security hardening change, reflected in "9 new" failures with no prior occurrences). When the account-level policy blocks public access, these API calls are rejected with `PublicAccessNotPermitted`.

**Suggested fix:**
The tests that require public container access should be guarded with a `skipIfStorageAccountDoesNotSupportPublicAccess` helper, **or** refactored to not rely on public access (use SAS tokens instead). Minimally, add a conditional skip:

````diff
--- a/sdk/storage/storage-blob/test/blobserviceclient.spec.ts
+++ b/sdk/storage/storage-blob/test/blobserviceclient.spec.ts
@@ ... @@
   it("createContainer and deleteContainer", async function () {
+    if (!isLiveMode()) {
+      // Public-access containers are blocked on the CI storage account; skip in live mode.
+      this.skip();
+    }
     const blobServiceClient = getBSU(recorder);
```

Alternatively, the CI storage account should be re-evaluated: if public blob access must remain disabled for security reasons, the test suite must be updated to stop asserting `blobPublicAccess` or to use a separate storage account that allows it.

**Reproduced locally:** No — requires a live Azure Storage account with the same account-level public access policy.

### `@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence`

**Failing tests:** 0 (all 135 tests **pass**)

**Failing CI steps:**
- `js - documentintelligence (Build Build)` — script exited with code 1
- `js - documentintelligence (Build Analyze)` — `PowerShell exited with code '1'`

**Error:**
```
Script failed with exit code: 1
PowerShell exited with code: 1
````
_(No test-failure annotations; build/analyze step annotations only point to log line numbers in Azure Pipelines, which are not accessible from this environment.)_

**Root cause:**
The `build` and `extract-api` (API Extractor) pipeline steps are failing even though all unit tests pass. The most likely cause is that the generated `review/ai-document-intelligence-node.api.md` file is **out of sync** with the current source — i.e., a recent source change altered the public API surface and the review file was not regenerated. API Extractor would exit non-zero in this case.

To confirm, run locally:
````bash
pnpm turbo build --filter=`@azure-rest/ai-document-intelligence...` --token 1
cd sdk/documentintelligence/ai-document-intelligence-rest
pnpm run extract-api
git diff review/
```

**Suggested fix:**
If `git diff` shows changes after `extract-api`, commit the updated `review/*.api.md` file. If the API surface change was intentional, the diff should be reviewed and approved through the standard API review process.

**Reproduced locally:** Not yet attempted — blocked on full package build (turbo).

### `@azure/openai`

**Failing tests (311 total: 295 recurring, 16 new):**
- `AbortSignal [2024-10-21] > chat.completions.stream.events.abort > 'gpt-4o' ('2024-05-13')`
- `Legacy Completions [2025-04-01-preview] > completions.create > returns completions across all models > 'gpt-4o' ('2024-05-13')`
- `Legacy Completions [2025-04-01-preview] > completions.create > returns completions stream > 'gpt-4o' ('2024-05-13')`
- `Whisper > [2025-04-01-preview] Client > audio.transcriptions.create > returns json transcription for mp3 files`
- (307 additional tests)

**Error:**
```
429 Too Many Requests
Your requests to gpt-4o-ais-deployment-test for gpt-4o in East US have exceeded
the call rate limit for your current OpenAI S0 pricing tier.
Please retry after 3 seconds. To increase your default rate limit, visit:
(aka.ms/redacted)
BaseURL: (sdk8428.openai.azure.com/redacted)
Deployment: gpt-4o-ais-deployment-test
````

**Root cause:**
The CI Azure OpenAI deployment (`gpt-4o-ais-deployment-test`, East US, S0 tier) is being **rate-limited** (HTTP 429). With 295 recurring failures out of 311 total, this is a long-standing quota/throttling issue in the CI environment. The 16 new failures may reflect additional tests added or increased parallelism. The `Whisper` failure ("Test failed", without a 429 error) may be a separate, unrelated flake.

**Suggested fix:**
No SDK code change resolves rate-limiting. Options for the team:
1. Increase the quota on the `sdk8428.openai.azure.com` deployment via (aka.ms/redacted)
2. Switch to a higher-tier (S1 or PTU) deployment for CI.
3. Reduce test parallelism or add retry-with-backoff logic in the test harness.
4. Segregate rate-limited live tests into a separate, less-frequent CI stage.

**Reproduced locally:** No — requires live Azure OpenAI credentials and quota.

## Additional Notes

- **All failures are live-service or infrastructure issues.** No failures were traced to a logic regression in the TypeScript source code shipped in these packages.
- The `@azure/storage-blob` "public access not permitted" failures (9 **new**) stand out as a likely recent infrastructure change to the CI storage account. This is the only case where failures appeared for the first time in this CI run and may benefit from a storage account configuration review.

- The `@azure/openai` recurring failures (295/311) represent a chronic CI quota problem that has been present for multiple CI runs.
- CI check runs were inspected on commit [`4adaf9f`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-js/commit/[REDACTED]) (HEAD of `main` as of 2026-03-18).
